summary: Data layer is well-designed and safety-focused. Uses immutable ropey::Rope for text storage with pure functional operations. No ORM, SQL, or database detected. All buffer operations return new instances rather than mutating in-place. Type-safe error handling throughout. Main concerns: large state snapshots in undo/redo stack and potential memory overhead from rope cloning.

recommendations.title: Implement undo stack memory limits
recommendations.description: Undo snapshots store full buffer clones. On large files with frequent edits, undo_stack could consume significant memory. Implement a configurable maximum snapshot count (e.g., 1000 snapshots) with FIFO eviction.

recommendations.title: Add buffer change tracking for incremental undo
recommendations.description: Currently each undo snapshot copies the entire buffer. Consider tracking only deltas (inserted/deleted ranges) to reduce memory footprint and improve performance on large files.

recommendations.title: Implement file format detection and normalization
recommendations.description: Buffer currently stores raw bytes. Add detection for line ending styles (CRLF vs LF) and file encoding. Display detected format in status bar and allow normalization on save.

recommendations.title: Add incremental backup snapshots
recommendations.description: On file save, optionally create a timestamped backup (e.g., ~/.config/alfred/backups/). Implement configurable retention (e.g., keep 10 most recent backups).
